notes.note: The work Cicero was composing at his seaside villas, which he calls by its Greek title politika --- the De Re Publica, his dialogue on the ideal commonwealth, then heavy going and still in progress.

notes.anchor: your son and mine, your Cicero
notes.note: Quintus's son, young Quintus Cicero (Cicero's nephew), whose education Cicero promises to oversee daily while his father is away in Gaul.
Cicero's own son Marcus, then a boy; teaching him has given Cicero some practice for tutoring his nephew.
Atella, an Oscan town in Campania between Capua and Naples, was under the patronage (in fide) of the Ciceros; M. Orfius, the man here commended, came from it.

notes.anchor: He is a military tribune in your army
notes.note: The tribunus militum was a senior officer of a legion, six to a legion; Orfius held this rank in Caesar's Gallic army, where Quintus was serving as a legate.
